entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan, the primary crop studied for adaptation in the short rainy season of Sri Lanka

entities.name: EXTRA-SHORT-DURATION
entities.description: A trait characterized by a very short growth cycle, allowing the crop to mature quickly

entities.name: SHORT-DURATION
entities.description: A trait characterized by a short growth cycle

entities.name: SHORT RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The yala season in Sri Lanka, occurring from late March to late May

entities.name: SRI LANKA
entities.description: The tropical bimodal rainfall environment where the study was conducted

entities.name: SUPPLEMENTARY IRRIGATION
entities.description: The application of additional water to crops to enhance growth and yield

entities.name: ICPL 88039
entities.description: An extra-short-duration genotype of pigeonpea that showed high yield and partitioning efficiency

entities.name: ICPL 86012
entities.description: A short-duration genotype of pigeonpea used in the study

entities.name: SESAME
entities.description: Sesamum indicum, a crop used for comparison under rainfed zero-tillage conditions

entities.name: BLACKGRAM
entities.description: Vigna mungo, a crop used for comparison under rainfed zero-tillage conditions

entities.name: RAINFED ZERO-TILLAGE
entities.description: A farming condition involving no irrigation and minimal soil disturbance, typically following a rice crop

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop grown prior to the sowing of pigeonpea, blackgram, or sesame in the study

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The quantity of seeds produced per unit area, used as a measure of crop performance

entities.name: GENOTYPE BY ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The phenomenon where different genotypes respond differently to varying environmental conditions

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE PERIOD
entities.description: The duration of the plant's reproductive phase, which influences adaptation to the short rainy season

entities.name: PARTITIONING EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of a genotype to allocate photosynthates to the seed, affecting overall yield
